## A mix of possion data and negative binomial data
```{r}

data <- rpois(1000, lambda = 4)

hist(data,
     breaks = 30,
     main = "Simulated Poisson Distribution",
     xlab = "Value")

lambda <- 4
x <- 0:15

y <- dpois(x, lambda)

plot(x, y, type = "l", lwd = 2,
     main = "Poisson Distribution (lambda = 4)",
     xlab = "x",
     ylab = "P(X = x)")
points(x, y, pch = 16)

library(ggplot2)

lambda <- 4
x <- 0:15
df <- data.frame(x = x, prob = dpois(x, lambda))

ggplot(df, aes(x, prob)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Poisson Distribution (λ = 4)",
       x = "x",
       y = "P(X = x)")

geom_col()

set.seed(1)

# simulate Poisson data
data <- rpois(1000, lambda = 4)

# histogram
h <- hist(data, breaks = seq(-0.5, max(data)+0.5, 1),
          col = "lightgray",
          main = "Histogram with Poisson Fit",
          xlab = "x")

# x values
x <- 0:max(data)

# Poisson probabilities scaled to histogram
y <- dpois(x, lambda = mean(data)) * length(data)

# overlay points and lines
points(x, y, col = "red", pch = 19)
lines(x, y, col = "red", lwd = 2)

x = seq(0, 15, 0.1)
y = dnorm(x, mean = 7)
plot(x, 2*y, type="l")
lines(x, y, col = "red", lwd = 2)
sum((x[2]-x[1])*y)
sum((x[2]-x[1])*2*y)
```
```{r}
set.seed(123)

n <- 5000
x <- runif(n, 0, 3)

beta0 <- 0.5
beta1 <- 0.8

mu <- exp(beta0 + beta1 * x)

# Left: Poisson data
y_left <- rpois(n, lambda = mu)

# Right: Negative Binomial data
size <- 2 # dispersion parameter
y_right <- rnbinom(n, mu = mu, size = size)

data <- data.frame(
    x = x,
    poisson_part = y_left,
    negbin_part = y_right
)


mean(y_left)
var(y_left)

mean(y_right)
var(y_right)


hist(y_left, main = "Poisson Data")
hist(y_right, main = "Negative Binomial Data")

print(list(
    mean_x = mean(x),
    mean_y_left = mean(y_left),
    mean_y_right = mean(y_right),
    var_x = var(x),
    var_y_left = var(y_left),
    var_y_right = var(y_right)
))
print(list(head = head(data), dim = dim(data)))
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)

n <- 500
x <- runif(n, 0, 3)

beta0 <- 0.5
beta1 <- 0.8

mu <- exp(beta0 + beta1 * x)

y <- numeric(n)

# Left half: Poisson
y[1:(n / 2)] <- rpois(n / 2, lambda = mu[1:(n / 2)])

# Right half: Negative Binomial
size <- 2
y[(n / 2 + 1):n] <- rnbinom(n / 2, mu = mu[(n / 2 + 1):n], size = size)

data <- data.frame(
    x = x,
    y = y,
    regime = c(rep("Poisson", n / 2), rep("NegBin", n / 2))
)

tapply(data$y, data$regime, mean)
tapply(data$y, data$regime, var)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(data, aes(x = x, y = y, color = regime)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6)
```


```{r}
y <- ifelse(
    x < 1.5,
    rpois(n, mu),
    rnbinom(n, mu = mu, size = size)
)

data <- data.frame(
    x = x,
    y = y,
    regime = ifelse(x < 1.5, "Poisson", "NegBin")
)

tapply(data$y, data$regime, mean)
tapply(data$y, data$regime, var)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(data, aes(x = x, y = y, color = regime)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6)
```
